observations.recommendation: 1.8	We recommended and the Local Chief Executive (LCE) agreed to direct the Local IATF to continuously enforce the existing health protocols implemented in the community and ensure that adequate resources are provided for related PPAs by the LGU.

2.10	We reiterated our prior years’ recommendation and the LCE agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant to (a) coordinate with the concerned bank officials to determine the facts surrounding the closure of the three bank accounts, based on which, take action in accordance with relevant regulations; and (b) exert extra efforts in locating the documents supporting the reconciling items and adjust books as warranted.

3.8	We recommended and the LCE agreed to direct the (a) Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to (i) coordinate with the source agencies for the reconciliation of records pertaining to the said fund transfers; (ii) facilitate the return of all unexpended balance of fund transfer to the source agency after the completion of the projects for the maximum utilization of funds; and (b) Municipal Engineer and head of other implementing offices to facilitate the implementation of the PPAs that remained unimplemented as at year-end.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to (a) create an Inventory Committee that shall (i) in coordination with the Property Division/Unit, plan/strategize on how to conduct and complete the physical inventory within the prescribed period; (ii) be temporarily relieved of  all their regular duties to devote their full time in the conduct of the physical inventory taking until the same is completed; (b) instruct all offices concerned, should the LGU take the option for the one-time cleansing of PPE accounts, to comply with the procedures prescribed COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020; and (c) direct the Municipal Assessor and Designated General Services Officer to facilitate the titling of the four parcels of land in the Municipality name.

5.9	We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the (a) Municipal Planning and Development Office (MPDO) and other concerned offices to (i) catch-up with the implementation of pending projects to avoid accumulation in the ensuing years; and (ii) maximize the utilization of the 20 percent development fund in order to comply with the requirement for optimal use and to help achieve desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes; and (b) Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Engineer to (i) include in its preparation of the Program of Works and Approved Budget for the Contract the CSHP as a separate pay item to promote the safety of the workers and the public; and (ii) regularly monitor the implementation of infrastructure projects for the timely identification of lapses in project implementation and the introduction of appropriate interventions.

6.5	We recommended and the LCE agreed to (a) maximize the use of LDDRMF in a manner consistent with regulations; and (b) fill in the vacant plantilla position of LDRRMO Assistant and fully capacitate the MDRRM Office once PS limitation permits, in compliance with Section 12(b) of the Republic Act No. 10121 and the NDRRMC/DILG/DBM JMC No. 2014-1.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the GFPS Executive Committee to (a) continue in adhering to the provisions of the Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 dated January 15, 2013 and; (b) seek acknowledgment from DILG on any changes or re-appropriation of future GPBs.

8.15	We recommended and the LCE agreed to (a) coordinate with the Sanggunian Bayan for the prioritization of the review and approval of the LGU’s Ecological Profile; and (b) instruct the Municipal Development Council to: (i) prioritize and fast track the preparation of MCLUP before the end of the three-year period of the elected officials and; (ii) henceforth ensure that the CDP and ELA are aligned with the completed MCLUP.

9.4	We commended the efforts of management and recommended, on which the LCE agreed, to instruct the Accounting Office to continuously comply with the existing tax laws and regulations particularly on the withholding of taxes and remittance thereof within the prescribed period.

10.4	We commended the efforts of management and recommended, on which the LCE agreed, to instruct the Accounting Office to continuously comply with the existing rules and regulations on the withholding of GSIS contributions and loan amortization due from employees and remittance thereof including government share within the prescribed period.

11.3	We commended the efforts of management and recommended, on which the LCE agreed, to instruct the Accounting Office to continuously comply with the existing rules and regulations on the withholding of PhilHealth contributions and loan amortization due from employees and remittance thereof within the prescribed period.

12.4	We commended the efforts of management and recommended, on which the LCE agreed, to instruct the Accounting Office to continuously comply with the existing rules and regulations on the withholding of HDMF contributions and loan amortization due from employees and remittance thereof within the prescribed period.
13.5	We recommended and the LCE agreed to require all persons liable to settle immediately the audit disallowances.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Local Chief Executive (LCE) agreed to require the (a) Municipal Accountant to (i) coordinate with the concerned bank officials to determine the facts surrounding the closure of the three bank accounts, based on which, take action in accordance with relevant regulations; (ii) exert extra efforts in locating the documents supporting the reconciling items including the stale checks, and adjust books as warranted; (iii) henceforth immediately take up in the books any cash reconciling item identified; and (b) Municipal Treasurer to send written notice to the payees at least one month before the checks become stale.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to: (a) find ways how to comply with the prescribed physical validation and reporting procedures for PPE with consideration of health requirements during the COVID-19 pandemic; (b) instruct the Municipal Assessor and Designated General Services Officer to facilitate the titling of the four parcels of land in the Municipality name; (c) require the designated General Service Officer and Municipal Accountant to (i) ensure that all government property officially acquired by the Municipality are accounted for and taken up in the books; (ii) provide proof of ownership on the 31 vehicles as identified in audits; and (iii) see to it that all its motor vehicles are registered annually with the LTOand placed under GSIS insurance coverage.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive (a) instruct the Municipal Engineer to conform the isolation facility with the DOH and DILG standards; (b) require the MSWDO and the Municipal Accountant an explanation on the spoiled/rotten relief goods; and (c) require the MSWDO and the Municipal Accountant to complete the submission of Relief Distribution List and ensure that all similar distributions are adequately and completely documented to promote transparency and facilitate establishment of accountability on the said activity.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the (a) General Services Officer to (i) perform the duties and responsibilities mandated under Section 490 (b) of the Local Government Code RA 7160; (ii) render report on the donations received by her Office; (b) Head, Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction Management Office (MDRRMO) to furnish the Accounting Office and Committee on Physical Inventory of PPE, a copy of Registry of Donated Relief Goods for DRRM and Acknowledgement Receipts for In-kind Donations for DRRM as bases of recording in the agency’s books and reports; (c) Municipal Accountant to (i) record In-kind Donations received; (ii) disclose in the Notes to Financial Statements of information pertaining to DRRM particularly on the quantity of donated relief goods received, issued and balance as well as the receipt of donated PPE (if any) and its disposal/transfer; and (d) Committee on Physical Inventory of PPE to include those In-kind Donations received by the municipality in the physical count and render its corresponding Inventory Report.

We recommended and the LCE concurred to instruct the Municipal Engineer to (a) ensure that the ongoing PPAS as at year-end are completed within the approved contract duration; and (b) continuously observe the timely implementation of development projects.

6.6	We recommended and the LCE concurred to require the: (a) Municipal Engineer to (i) determine and address the cause of the deficiencies pertaining to detailed engineering; (ii) ensure the completion of the projects within the time frame specified in the contract and take appropriate action against the erring contractor, as warranted; (iii) provide the Audit Team with the necessary documents that will support time extension due to Extra Work Order; and (iv) submit an explanation on the cost of un-complied COSH Programs; (b) BAC and TWG to (i) undertake bidding and award only after the detailed engineering for the project had been sufficiently carried out as provided in IRR of RA 9184; (ii) scrutinize the sufficiency of the contractor’s manpower on all the projects in the post-qualification stage of the procurement process; and (c) Municipal Accountant to ensure that the required documents are attached to the Disbursement Vouchers before processing of payments.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to (a) fully capacitate the LDRRM Office and comply with Section 12(b) of the Republic Act No. 10121 and all its legal requirements once PS limitation permits; (b) expedite recruitment for the vacant position at the LDRRMO for a more efficient and facilitated delivery of disaster responses;  and (c) consider detailing personnel from other departments to assist the DRRM Officer.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the (a) GFPS Executive Committee to continue its efforts in adhering to the provisions of the Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 dated January 15, 2013 and; (b) Municipal Planning Development Office Head to monitor the status of maintenance/updating the GAD database.

We commended the efforts of management and recommended, on which the LCE agreed, to instruct the Accounting Office to continue its compliance with the BIR regulations on withholding of taxes and remittance thereof within the prescribed period.

We commended the efforts of management and recommended, on which the LCE agreed, to instruct the Accounting Office to continue its compliance with law and regulations on withholding of GSIS dues from employees and the timely remittance thereof.

We commended the efforts of management, especially the Accounting Office, and recommended, on which the LCE agreed, to continue its compliance with the existing rules and regulations on withholding of Philhealth premium contributions of employees and remittance thereof within the prescribed period and recommended that they sustain their very satisfactory observance of related regulations.

We commended the efforts of management, especially the Accounting Office, and recommended, on which the LCE agreed, to continue its compliance with the existing rules and regulations on withholding of HDMF dues from employees and the timely remittance thereof.
We recommended and the LCE agreed to require all persons liable to settle immediately the audit disallowances.

We reiterated our prior years’ recommendation and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive  

(a) instruct the Municipal Accountant to settle and record all reconciling items in the bank reconciliation statements immediately in order to reflect the accurate balance of Cash in Bank accounts; and 

(b) continue to exert efforts to obtain the necessary documents as a support for the possible write-off of the closed bank accounts.

We recommended and management agreed to 

(a) seek the approval prior to spending the allocation for capital outlay in their succeeding projects from the PCW; 

(b) prioritize capacitating the designated VAWC personnel through seminars and trainings; and 

(c) direct all concerned Punong Barangays to immediately establish an active VAWC Desk in their respective barangays.

We recommended and the management agreed that the Local Chief Executive 

(a) instruct the 

(ii) heads of implementing offices to expedite the implementation and completion of development projects that were not yet started/completed as at December 31, 2017; and

(c) henceforth, strictly comply with the provisions of Section 5 of the DILG and DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017.

We recommended and management agreed  that the Local Chief Executuve instruct the 

(a) MPD Officer and the Municipal Engineer to 

(i) fast track the completion of the partially-implemented BUB projects; and 

(ii)coordinate with the funding agency to work out the continued funding of unimplemented BUB projects; and 

(b) Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to prepare  and post in the PhilGEPs website the Procurement Monitoring in accordance with Sec 12.2 of the revised IRR of RA 9184.

1.8	We reiterated our prior years’ recommendation and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant to seek the assistance of the depository banks to recapture or reconstitute prior years’ bank transactions as a take-off action in the identification and documentation of the (a) reconciling items that had been left unrecorded in the books for so long; and (b) the three bank accounts that had already been closed.

2.7	We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to (a) instruct the Local Development Council and the offices concerned to (i) determine and accordingly address the causes of the non/delayed implementation of the projects; (ii) recommend for the immediate implementation of the same, as warranted; and (b) ensure that government funds are timely and efficiently utilized to promote a facilitated delivery of public service.

5.5	We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to require the Municipal Accountant to impose the mandatory Net Take Home Pay to all Municipal employees by limiting the payroll deductions to the order of preference provided in the GAA.
11.6	We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to require all persons liable to settle immediately the audit disallowances.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive should 

(a) prioritize the conduct of physical count of assets of the LGU in accordance with the prescribed procedures and to render a report thereon in the manner and frequency required by regulations; and 

(b) direct the GSO and the Accountant to update and maintain the required PPE records, and reports for ready verification of the same.

We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to 

(a) effect the the adjustment of Loans Payable-Domestic (Current and Non-current) account to reflect the correct amount presented in the financial statements; and 

(b) maintain separate subsidiary ledgers for loans payable and interest payments for every loan obtained to facilitate monitoring of loan releases, principal and interest payments; and 

(c) for future-related transactions, adhere strictly with the provision of above mentioned PPSAS and COA Circular No. 2015-009.

We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive 

(a) direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to observe the due dates of submission of the financial reports and documents to the Audit Team and strictly comply with the timely rendition of accounts to facilitate verification and post-audit activities; and 

(b) impose sanctions against the accountable officers who continuously failed to abide with the timely submission of financial reports and documents.

We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive 

(a) instruct all department heads involved in development projects to monitor and evaluate the implementation of the proposed programs and projects; and 

(b) to ensure that the allocated funds are optimally utilized for projects identified in the approved AIP, in accordance with the guidelines/policies prescribed under the DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017.

We recommended and management agreed that the 

(a) Disaster Risk Reduction Management (DRRM) Council 

(i) optimize the utilization of the LDRRMF of the municipality; 

(ii) give priority to the procurement of basic rescue and response equipment and the conduct a municipal-wide training; 


(iii) decide on the use of the unexpended balance of LDRRMF which shall be incorporated in the LDRRMF Investment Plan; and 

(iv) request authority from the Sangguniang Bayan for authority for the Municipal Mayor and Municipal Treasurer to open a separate bank account solely for the unspent LDRRM Fund; and 

(b) DRRM Officer and Municipal Accountant to submit regularly the report on sources and utilization of DRRMF on or before the 15th day after the end of each month.

We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive require the concerned employee to prepare the Monthly Report of Official Travels, Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption and duly accomplished Driver's Trip Tickets for all vehicles owned by the municipal government pursuant to COA Circular No. 77-61 dated September 26, 1977 in order to monitor vehicle utilization efficiently.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive 

(c) withhold salaries of accountable officers who fail to effect prompt liquidation of their outstanding cash advances; and 

(d) institute appropriate administrative proceedings in case of non-compliance.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive to instruct 

(a) the Inventory Committee to conduct a comprehensive inventory count and prepare a complete report for submission to the Office of the Auditor; 

(b) the General Services Office and the Accounting Unit to prepare and maintain property records such as subsidiary records and ledgers and property cards respectively and conduct the periodic reconciliation of these records; and 

(c) the Accounting Unit to update the lapsing schedule of Property, Plant and Equipment by providing the lacking necessary information.

We recommended and management agreed  that the Local Chief Executuve instruct the 

(a) MPD Officer and the Municipal Engineer to (i) fast track the completion of the partially-implemented BUB projects; and (ii)coordinate with the funding agency to work out the continued funding of unimplemented BUB projects; and 

(b) Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to prepare  and post in the PhilGEPs website the Procurement Monitoring in accordance with Sec 12.2 of the revised IRR of RA 9184.

1.7	We reiterated our prior years’ recommendation and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive  (a) instruct the Municipal Accountant to settle and record all reconciling items in the bank reconciliation statements immediately in order to reflect the accurate balance of Cash in Bank accounts; and (b) continue to exert efforts to obtain the necessary documents as a support for the possible write-off of the closed bank accounts.

2.6	We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive should (a) prioritize the conduct of physical count of assets of the LGU in accordance with the prescribed procedures and to render a report thereon in the manner and frequency required by regulations; and (b) direct the GSO and the Accountant to update and maintain the required PPE records, and reports for ready verification of the same.

3.7	We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to (a) effect the the adjustment of Loans Payable-Domestic (Current and Non-current) account to reflect the correct amount presented in the financial statements; and (b) maintain separate subsidiary ledgers for loans payable and interest payments  for every loan obtained to facilitate monitoring of loan releases, principal and interest payments; and (c) for future-related transactions, adhere strictly with the provision of above mentioned PPSAS and COA Circular No. 2015-009.

4.6	We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive (a) direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to observe the due dates of submission of the financial reports and documents to the Audit Team and strictly comply with the timely rendition of accounts to facilitate verification and post-audit activities; and (b) impose sanctions against the accountable officers who continuously failed to abide with the timely submission of financial reports and documents.

5.14	We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive (a) instruct all department heads involved in development projects to monitor and evaluate the implementation of the proposed programs and projects; and (b) to ensure that the allocated funds are optimally utilized for projects identified in the approved AIP, in accordance with the guidelines/policies prescribed under the DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017.

7.16	We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive, through the Bids and Awards Committee, (a) consider the general policy of the government in acquiring brand new equipment after appropriate consideration of availability of funds; (b) fully consider the general obsolescence of the transport and heavy equipment, the availability of spare parts, and the quality, degree and extent   of repair and maintenance requirements in the acquisition of second-hand/reconditioned equipment; (c) conduct technical study before entering into purchase agreement of second-hand vehicle and; (d) strictly adhere to DBM NBC 446-A in the procurement of the second-hand motor vehicles.

8.17	We recommended and management agreed that the (a) Disaster Risk Reduction Management (DRRM) Council (i) optimize the utilization of the LDRRMF of the municipality; (ii) give priority to the procurement of basic rescue and response equipment and the conduct a municipal-wide training; (iii) decide on the use of the unexpended balance of LDRRMF which shall be incorporated in the LDRRMF Investment Plan; and (iv) request authority from the Sangguniang Bayan for authority for the Municipal Mayor and Municipal Treasurer to open a separate bank account solely for the unspent LDRRM Fund; and (b) DRRM Officer and Municipal Accountant to submit regularly the report on sources and utilization of DRRMF on   or before the 15th day after the end of each month.

10.8	We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive require the concerned employee to prepare the Monthly Report of Official Travels, Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption and duly accomplished Driver's Trip Tickets for all vehicles owned by the municipal government pursuant to COA Circular No. 77-61 dated September 26, 1977 in order to monitor vehicle utilization efficiently.

11.10	We recommended and management agreed to (a) seek the approval prior to spending the allocation for capital outlay in their succeeding projects from the PCW; (b) prioritize capacitating the designated VAWC personnel through seminars and trainings; and (c) direct all concerned Punong Barangays to immediately establish an active VAWC Desk in their respective barangays.

12.5	We recommended and management agreed that the Municipal Accountant continue to faithfully comply with its duty to withhold taxes due on compensation and all money payments to suppliers, contractors and other creditors and promptly remit the same to the Bureau of Internal Revenue in order to contribute to the government’ revenue-generation efforts.
13.2	We recommended and management agreed that the Municipality continue to deduct the mandatory contribution and loan amortizations and to promptly remit the same within the prescribed period.

14.2	We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to continuously comply with the existing rules and regulations on withholding of HDMF contributions and loan amortization from salary of personnel and on remittance thereof to the HDMF within the prescribed period.

15.6	We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive cause the immediate submission by the proper COA Office of the documents supporting their alleged settlements made for the latter's validation.

We reiterated our prior years’ recommendation and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive 

(a) instruct the Municipal Accountant to settle and record all reconciling items in the bank reconciliation statements immediately in order to reflect the accurate balance of Cash in Bank accounts; and 

(b) continue to exert efforts to obtain the necessary documents to resolve the issues on the closed bank accounts.
observations.managementAction: Request letter was submitted to the Municipal Treasury Office to retrieve DVs and supporting documents of payments made not submitted in the accounting office for recording. Continuously tracing available records for reconciliation.
observations.reason: Reiterated under Observation No. 1, AAR CY 2018

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our prior year's recommendation and management agreed that the 

(a) Disaster Risk Reduction Management (DRRM) Officer regularly monitor and evaluate the utilization of Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Fund (LDRRMF) based on the PPAs listed in the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Plan (LDRRMP); 

(b) Budget Officer and Municipal Accountant to stop processing claims that are not related to disaster preparation, mitigation and preparedness; and 

(c) DRRM Officer and Municipal Accountant to submit regularly the report on sources and utilization of DRRMF on or before the 15th day after the end of each month.

We recommended and the management agreed that the Local Chief Executive and Local School Board 

(a) prepare a comprehensive annual plan and budget for SEF with adequate consideration of the actual, realistic and more pressing needs of the public school sector; and 

(b) monitor the implementation of PPAs vis-à-vis the milestones and timelines set to ensure optimum use of the fund.

We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive 

(a) cause the immediate development and operation of a sanitary landfill as the final waste disposal; 

(b) instruct the MENR Designate to ensure compliance to sanitation standards of the temporary residual containment area being the present central disposal site and the barangay MRFs; and 

(c) provide the necessary legislative support in the implementation of waste segregation at source, composting, recycling and applicable control measures on waste management.

We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive, as Chairman of the GAD Focal Point System (GFPS), instruct the GAD Council and GFPS members to 

(a) continue coordinating efforts among implementing offices of the Municipality to ensure full implementation of the GAD’s programs and activities; and 

(b) make regular follow-up with the oversight bodies for the timely review and approval of succeeding years’ GAD Plans and Budgets.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the Municipal Mayor and the concerned persons responsible cause the immediate settlement of the audit suspensions, otherwise a Notice of Disallowance will be issued in lieu of the matured NS.  Similarly, require the liable officials and employees to refund the disallowance otherwise cause the withholding of their salaries or any money claim.

We recommend the Local Chief Executive to instruct the Municipal Accountant settle and record all reconciling items in the bank reconciliation statements  immediately in order to reflect the accurate balance of Cash in Bank accounts. We also reiterated our prior year's recommendation that the Municipal Accountant continue to exert efforts to obtain the necessary documents to resolve the issues on the closed bank accounts.

We recommended that the local cheif executive instruct 

(a) the inventory committee to prepare a comprehensive inventory count and prepare a complete report for submission to the office of the auditor; 

(b) the general services office and the accounting unit to prepare and maintain property records such as subsidiary records and ledgers and property cards respectively and both offices should conduct the  periodic reconciliation of these records; and 

(c) the accounting unit to update the lapsing schedule of Property, Plant and Equipment by providing the lacking necessary information.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive:

(a) coordinate efforts with barangay officials and implement continuously the five basic environmental laws on preserving and protecting the environment expressly stated under R.A. No. 9003; and 

(b) instruct the Designated Municipal Environment and Natural Resources Officer to monitor the establishment and operations of barangay MRFs and provide the necessary support in the implementation of waste segregation at source and applicable control measures on waste management at the barangay level.

We reiterated our prior years' recommendation that management should fully comply with the provisions of Joint Memorandum Circular N0. 2013-01 PCW, DILG, DBM and NEDA by: 

(a) attributing at least five percent (5%) of the total budget appropriation to fund the implementation of GAD programs, projects and activities; and 

(b) enhancing its capability of identifying and analysing dender issues and formulating GAD PPAs to address satisfactorily them.

We recommended and management agreed that the Municipal Ecological Solid Waste Management Board 

(a) exert effort to expedite the establishment of the Sanitary Landfill; 

(b) involve closely the Barangay officials in the strict implementation on the existing environmental laws and intensify the information campaign on the “no segregation, no collection policy and the “no-littering policy” among the residents; and 

(c)  exercise strong political will to implement the environmental laws on preserving and protecting the environment as mandated under Republic Act  No. 9003.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the Municipal Mayor and the concerned persons responsible cause the immediate settlement of the audit suspensions, otherwise a Notice of Disallowance will be issued in lieu of the matured NS.  Similarly, require the liable officials and employees to refund the disallowance otherwise cause the withholding of their salaries or any money claim.

1.8	We recommended the Local Chief Executive to instruct the Municipal Accountant settle and record all reconciling items in the bank reconciliation statements immediately in order to reflect the accurate balance of Cash in Bank accounts.
1.9	We also reiterated our prior years’ recommendation that the Municipal Accountant continue to exert efforts to obtain the necessary documents to resolve the issues on the closed bank accounts.

2.9	We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation to: (a) send demand letters to the concerned accountable officers for the immediate liquidation of their accountabilities including those who are no longer connected with the LGU; (b) stop the practice of granting new cash advances without the liquidation of the previous accountability and require the concerned personnel to liquidate their cash advances as soon as the purpose thereof has been accomplished; (c) withhold salaries of accountable officers who fail to effect prompt liquidation of their outstanding cash advances; and (d) institute appropriate administrative proceedings in case of non-compliance.

3.10	We recommended that the: (a) Disaster Risk Reduction Management (DRRM) Officer regularly monitor and evaluate the utilization of Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Fund (LDRRMF) based on the PPAs listed in the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Plan (LDRRMP); (b) Budget Officer and Municipal Accountant to stop processing claims not related to disaster preparation, mitigation and preparedness; and (c) DRRM and Municipal Accountant prepare and submit regularly the report on sources and utilization of DRRMF on or before the 15th day after the end of each month.

4.11	We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct (a) the Inventory Committee to prepare a comprehensive inventory count and prepare a complete report for submission to the Office of the Auditor; (b) the General Services Office and the Accounting Unit to prepare and maintain property records such as subsidiary records and ledgers and property cards respectively and conduct the periodic reconciliation of these records; and (c) the Accounting Unit to update the lapsing schedule of Property, Plant and Equipment by providing the lacking necessary information.

5.9	We recommended that Members of the Municipal Local School Board: (a) prepare a balanced budget with conscious and concerted efforts of observing compliance with the legal and regulatory requirements on SEF budgeting, allocation and utilization and stop the practice of accommodating expenditures for activities for which the DepEd has the direct mandate to execute; (b) request for inputs from the Deped official/School Division Superintendent in the prioritization of SEF projects and expenditures; (c) maximize the utilization of SEF to attain its objectives, monitor the implementation of the Project Program Activities (PPA’s), and take appropriate actions such as realignment or reprograming, if necessary.

6.12	We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: (a) ensure the full or optimum utilization of the 20% Development Fund during the budget year; (b) instruct the Municipal Planning and Development Officer (MPDO) to abide strictly with the guidelines in the formulation of the 20% Development Plan; (c) instruct the responsible officers to revert or reprogram the fund from unimplemented projects which are not among those enumerated to be chargeable to 20% DF and reprogram the same for qualified items and; (d) direct the members of the BAC to schedule early all procurement activities pertaining to development projects to pave way for their timely implementation and completion.

7.10	We recommended that the Local Chief Executive: (a) coordinate efforts with barangay officials and implement continuously the five basic environmental laws on preserving and protecting the environment expressly stated under R.A. No. 9003; and (b) instruct the Designated Municipal Environment and Natural Resources Officer to monitor the establishment and operations of barangay MRFs and provide the necessary support in the implementation of waste segregation at source and applicable control measures on waste management at the barangay level.

8.6	We reiterated our prior years’ recommendation that management should fully comply with the provisions of Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 PCW, DILG, DBM and NEDA by: (a) attributing at least five percent (5%) of the total budget appropriation to fund the implementation of GAD programs, projects and activities; and (b) enhancing its capability of identifying and analysing gender issues and formulating GAD PPAs to address them satisfactorily.

9.5	We recommended that the Municipal Accountant (a) continue to faithfully comply with its duty to withhold taxes due on compensation and all money payments to suppliers, contractors and other creditors and promptly remit the same to the Bureau of Internal Revenue in order to contribute to the government’ revenue-generation efforts; and (b) comply strictly with Revenue Memorandum Circular No. 23-2007 and BIR Tax Revenue Regulation No. 10-2008.
10.3	We recommended that the Municipality continue to promptly remit the withheld GSIS contributions/premiums/loan amortizations from employees compensations within the prescribed period.

11.5	We recommended that the Municipal Mayor (a) cause the immediate settlement of all outstanding audit suspensions and disallowances; and (b) require the responsible officials and employees/persons liable to refund their disallowance.

We recommended and management agreed that all reconciling items in the bank reconciliation statements be systematically followed up and immediately cleared by the Municipal Accountant.  Necessary adjusting entries should be effected in the books to reflect the accurate balance of Cash in Bank accounts.

We also reiterated our prior years’ recommendation that the Municipal Accountant continue to exert efforts to obtain the necessary documents in order to prepare bank reconciliation statements and reconcile the balance retained in the books on all closed bank accounts.

We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive instruct (a) the Inventory Committee to prepare a comprehensive inventory count and prepare a complete report for submission to the Office of the Auditor; (b) the General Services Office and the Accounting Unit to prepare and maintain property records such as subsidiary records and ledgers and property cards respectively and both offices should conduct periodic reconciliation of these records; and (c) the Accounting Unit to update the lapsing schedule of Property, Plant and Equipment by providing the lacking necessary information.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation and management agreed to (a) send demand letters to the concerned accountable officers for the immediate liquidation of their accountabilities; exert efforts to locate the accountable officers/persons no longer connected with the LGU; (b) stop the practice of granting new cash advances without the liquidation of previous ones and require the concerned personnel to liquidate their cash advances as soon as the purpose thereof had been accomplished; (c) withhold salaries of accountable officers who fail to effect prompt liquidation of their outstanding cash advances; and (d) institute appropriate administrative proceedings in case of non-compliance.

We recommended and management agreed that the Municipal Treasurer to update the Real Property Tax Account Register in order to prepare the Certified List of Taxpayers and Taxes Due as basis of the Municipal Accountant in setting up  RPT/SET Receivable and Deferred RPT/SET Income at the beginning of each year.

We recommended that the Head of Agency (a) require the Accountable Officer to restitute immediately the missing funds; (b) file appropriate charges against the erring Accountable Officer if circumstances warrant; (c) withhold the salary or any money due to the Accountable Officer in satisfaction of his shortage; (d) relieve of the defaulting Accountable Officer from his duties as ICO-Municipal Treasurer; (e) transfer him to the position where money accountability is not involve; (f) direct the ICO-Municipal Treasurer to deposit intact and regularly all collections to its authorized depository bank pursuant to COA Circular No. 92-382 as well as future collections; (g) require the defaulting ICO-Municipal Treasurer to explain in writing why he failed to deposit his collections regularly and intact to its depository bank; (h) direct the ICO-Municipal Treasurer to stop the practice of using the collections to pay various disbursements of the Municipality; and (i) require the Municipal Accountant and the ICO-Municipal Treasurer to make monthly reconciliation of their subsidiary ledger and cashbook balances so that whatever differences/ deficiencies could be adjusted immediately.

We also recommended that the ICO-Municipal Treasurer to (a) surrender and present all accountable forms not presented during the cash examination and remit the corresponding collections; (b) direct all accountable officers to adhere to the policy of daily remittance or turn-over of their collections and remit all the unremitted collections to the Liquidating Officer; (c) adjust the errors noted in the cashbook to reflect the correct cashbook balance; (d) adhere to the guideline that all transactions shall be personally recorded by the Accountable Officer in the cashbook; (e) stop the practice of allowing other person to record transaction/s in your cashbook; and (f) require all collecting officers to obtain a Fidelity Bond Certificate from Bureau of Treasury.

We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive (a) direct the Municipal Planning and Development Officer to monitor the effective and efficient implementation of the projects under the 20% Development Fund so that timely necessary interventions by the implementing offices may be made for their timely completion; (b) advise the Local Development Council to perform due diligence in the review of the budget to ensure the proper funding source of identified high-impact or capital expenditure projects for optimum attainment of the intent of both the 20% Development Fund and the SEF.

We recommended and management agreed that members of Local School Board (a) prepare a balanced budget with conscious and concerted efforts of observing compliance with the legal and regulatory requirements on SEF budgeting, allocation and utilization; (b) request for inputs from the Deped official/School Division Superintendent in the prioritization of SEF projects and expenditures in accordance with the DepEd criteria and with due regard to the provisions of laws, rules and regulations prescribing the limitations on the use of the fund; (c) maximize the utilization of SEF to attain its objectives and take appropriate actions such as realignment or reprograming, if necessary; and (d) stop the practice of formulating the budget and using the fund which include non-priority items listed under the aforesaid laws, rules and regulations.

We recommended and management agreed that the (a) Municipal Accountant (i) continue to adhere with the proper accounting and reporting guidelines of COA Circular No. 2012-002; (ii) prepare a Journal Entry Voucher to reclassify the erroneous recording of equipment to its proper account; and (c) LDRRM Council refrain from budgeting and utilizing DRRMF for programs/projects/activities which are not directly related with disaster preparedness, mitigation, response and rescue operations.

We recommended and management agreed that the Municipal Ecological Solid Waste Management Board (a) exert effort to expedite the establishment of the Sanitary Landfill; (b) involve closely the Barangay officials in the strict implementation on the existing environmental laws and intensify the information campaign on the “no segregation, no collection policy and the “no-littering policy” among the residents; and (c)  exercise strong political will to implement the environmental laws on preserving and protecting the environment as mandated under Republic Act  No. 9003.

We recommended and management agreed to fully comply with the provisions of Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 of the Philippine Commission on Women (PCW), DILG, the DBM and NEDA by (a) setting aside of at least five percent (5%) of the total budget appropriation to cover the implementation of GAD programs, (b) identifying gender issues and designing training program toward women empowerment and gender equality.

We recommended and management agreed that the (a) Personnel Officer hire job order personnel based on necessity for a period not exceeding six months and (b) Municipal Accountant (i) support disbursements of funds for services rendered by job order personnel with the necessary and appropriate supporting documents to establish validity and propriety of payment; (ii) ensure that daily time records (DTR) are properly filled-up; and (iii) deductions are made on wages of job order employees with absences.

We recommended that management promptly remit to the BIR all of its taxes withheld and comply with its duty to withhold taxes due on compensation and all money payments to suppliers, contractors and other creditors in order to contribute to the government’s revenue-generation efforts.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the (a) Municipal Treasurer to follow strictly the provisions of Section 14.1 Rule III of Republic Act No. 8291 in order to avoid accumulation of payables to GSIS and incurrence of interests and penalties due to non or delayed remittance; and (b) Municipal Accountant to trace the actual date where the previous year’s unremitted premium contributions occurred and make necessary steps to settle the balance appearing on the record.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the Municipal Mayor and the concerned persons responsible cause the immediate settlement of the audit suspensions, otherwise a Notice of Disallowance will be issued in lieu of the matured NS.  Similarly, require the liable officials and employees to refund the disallowance otherwise cause the withholding of their salaries or any money claim.
